hero.heading: I came. I build.
hero.subtitle: I don't have a five-year plan. I have a list of things I can't stop thinking about, a job that's teaching me more than college ever did, and the stubbornness to keep moving.

milestones.title: LLM Chess Tournament
milestones.desc: I wanted to see what happens when you pit LLMs against each other in chess. Turns out, they're terrible — but watching Gemini blunder a queen against GPT-4 is genuinely entertaining. Built a whole ELO rating system around it.
milestones.aim: I just wanted to know: can these things actually think strategically?
ideas: Wired up Groq, Gemini, OpenAI to play each other
Built an ELO leaderboard from scratch
Move-by-move viewer so you can watch the chaos

milestones.title: Dream Home Design
milestones.desc: Took a break from AI and built something completely different — an interior design visualizer. Honestly? I needed to prove to myself I could ship a clean frontend without hiding behind a model API.
milestones.aim: Ship something that has nothing to do with AI. Just good UI.
ideas: Room configurator with drag-and-drop
Responsive layout that doesn't suck on mobile
Deployed to Vercel in one night

milestones.title: Got promoted at DiligenceVault
milestones.desc: Started as an AI Engineer Intern in October. Three months later they made me a Software Developer. I'm processing 5,000+ financial document chunks daily — the RAG pipeline I built hits 82% precision. Not perfect, but it cut manual review by 60%.
milestones.aim: Prove I can ship in a real team, not just in my room at 2am.
ideas: Built agent orchestration workflows from scratch
Delivered production REST APIs that people actually depend on
Learned what 'code review' really means when your senior has opinions

milestones.title: This portfolio
milestones.desc: I hated every portfolio template I saw. So I made this — film shutter transitions, a chatbot that talks like me, and a page where I publicly track my goals (you're looking at it). No rounded corners. That's a hill I'll die on.
milestones.aim: Make something that actually feels like me, not a Dribbble shot.
ideas: Film shutter page transitions because why not
Chatbot that's sarcastic on purpose
This Poreia page — accountability in public

milestones.title: TGG — Vectorless RAG
milestones.desc: This is the one I can't stop thinking about. Everyone does RAG the same way — chunk documents, embed them, cosine similarity, pray. I think that's broken. So I'm building something that parses documents into trees and navigates them deterministically. No embeddings. No vector DB. Just structure.
milestones.aim: I want to prove there's a better way to do retrieval. If I'm wrong, at least I'll understand why embeddings won.
ideas: Parse documents into actual trees, not chunks
3-step LLM query enrichment before retrieval
Deterministic fetch — no cosine similarity gambling
Learning Rust for this (it's humbling)
SQLite because vector DBs are overkill for this

milestones.title: Surviving production code
milestones.desc: Every day at DiligenceVault I ship something. Some days it's clean. Some days I stare at a PR review wondering how I ever thought that code was good. But I'm learning faster here than I did in four years of college.
milestones.aim: Become the engineer who doesn't need hand-holding. Not there yet.
ideas: Getting faster at debugging other people's code
Learning that 'it works' isn't the same as 'it's good'
CI/CD finally makes sense to me
My seniors are patient and I don't deserve it

milestones.title: Stop being invisible
milestones.desc: I build stuff but nobody knows. That's my fault. Started going to meetups, posting on LinkedIn (it feels weird), and actually talking to other engineers instead of just reading their tweets.
milestones.aim: If a tree falls in a forest and nobody hears it — that's my GitHub right now.
ideas: Showed up to two AI meetups (didn't die)
Started posting about TGG on LinkedIn
Replied to some open-source threads instead of just lurking
Still terrible at small talk

milestones.title: Actually fine-tune an LLM
milestones.desc: I've been calling myself an AI engineer but I've never trained a model from scratch. That bothers me. Planning to LoRA fine-tune something small on a domain I care about. Maybe academic papers, maybe financial docs from work.
milestones.aim: I need to understand what happens below the API layer. I can't keep being a 'prompt engineer with extra steps.'
ideas: LoRA/QLoRA because I don't have infinite GPUs
Curate a dataset that actually matters
Build an eval pipeline so I know if it's better or just different
Compare honestly against the base model

milestones.title: Give a talk (terrifying)
milestones.desc: I want to talk about TGG at a meetup or online conference. The thought makes my stomach drop. But if I can build a vectorless RAG system, I can survive 20 minutes on a stage. Probably.
milestones.aim: Say something useful to a room of people who know more than me. Try not to pass out.
ideas: Write a proposal that doesn't sound like a student presentation
Build a live demo that won't crash (famous last words)
Practice until I stop saying 'umm' every three seconds
Getting rejected is fine — the prep alone will be worth it

milestones.title: Open-source something real
milestones.desc: I want to extract something from TGG or this portfolio and actually package it for others. Not a tutorial repo with 3 stars — a real library with docs, tests, and a reason to exist.
milestones.aim: Stop only consuming open-source. Contribute something back that someone actually installs.
ideas: Figure out what's actually worth extracting
Write documentation that I'd want to read
Set up proper CI, versioning, changelog
Merge my first external PR without panicking

milestones.title: SLM Agentic Coding Tool
milestones.desc: This is the big dream. A coding assistant that runs on small language models, fully local, no cloud dependency. I use AI tools every day — I want to build one that works the way I think. Still mostly a napkin sketch right now.
milestones.aim: Build the tool I wish existed. If it's good enough for me, maybe it's good enough for others.
ideas: Find the right SLM that's fast enough to not be annoying
Agent loop with actual tool use, not just autocomplete
Everything runs locally — privacy isn't optional
IDE integration that doesn't feel bolted on

radar.title: Am I Good?
radar.subtitle: I sat down and rated myself honestly. It hurt a little. The blue line is where I think I'll be in eighteen months if I keep showing up. Some of these gaps keep me up at night.

skills.title: Where I Am → Where I Want to Be
skills.subtitle: Red is where I am. Blue is where I think I can get. The space between is eighteen months of not sleeping enough.
